title: list-tests output
description: JSON Schema for the stdout of scripts/list-tests/list-tests.js. The tool statically extracts (no compilation) a best-effort tasty test tree for every test suite reported by scripts/list-test-suites/list-test-suites.sh, using tree-sitter-haskell (WASM). This is the Tier-1 approximation; the authoritative tree comes from the streaming --list-tests-json output, which replaces this output entirely.

root.description: Absolute path to the scanned root directory in live output. Version-controlled golden files normalize this to the literal "<ROOT>" for portability; the test harness applies the same normalization to live output before diffing.

grammar.description: Path to the tree-sitter-haskell WASM grammar used for parsing, relative to root. Stable across machines.

suites.description: One entry per test suite discovered by the list-test-suites tool (optionally filtered by --suite).

package.description: Package directory containing the suite, relative to root.

entryFile.description: main-is entry file for the suite, relative to root, or the literal "MISSING" when the file could not be resolved.

entryPoint.description: Classification of the suite's main module, propagated from the list-test-suites tool: STREAMING (defaultMainStreaming / defaultMainTestingInterface), upstream (plain tasty defaultMain), unknown, or MISSING (main-is absent).

tree.description: Root node of the extracted test tree, or null when no tree could be extracted (e.g. MISSING entry file). See the `note` field for the reason.

note.description: Optional human-readable note explaining a null/partial tree or other caveat.

kind.description: group = a testGroup container; test = a leaf test case; placeholder = a node standing in for content that cannot be statically enumerated (e.g. dynamically generated content).

label.description: The node's label as written in the source. May be null when the label is not a string literal (a non-literal expression the static extractor cannot resolve).

source.description: Provenance of the node: parsed (read directly from the .hs source), synthesized (constructed by the extractor from a known pattern such as the TestingInterface tree), or dynamic (a placeholder for content produced at runtime).

testingInterface.description: Present and true on nodes that belong to a TestingInterface (propRunActions*) synthesized subtree (the top synthesized group and all of its descendants). Lets the extension render/treat them specially. Omitted on plain parsed/literal nodes.

role.description: Semantic role of a node within a TestingInterface subtree, for the extension to label/icon. positive = the "Positive tests" leaf; negative = the "Negative tests" leaf; threat-models-group = the "Threat models" group; expected-vulnerabilities-group = the "Expected vulnerabilities" group; threat-model = the individual leaves under BOTH groups (expected-vulnerability leaves are structurally identical to threat-model leaves, so they share role=threat-model; the parent group's role carries the distinction). Omitted on nodes without a special role.

pendingExpansion.description: Present and true on provisional nodes whose real/expanded content arrives only when the slow --list-tests command runs: unresolved cross-project/cross-package references, dynamic placeholders, the synthesized threat-model/expected-vulnerability leaves (whose real names from getThreatModelName we can't statically know), and the synthesized top group (names/shape change after --list-tests).

file.description: Source file the node was extracted from, relative to root. Stable across machines. Omitted on some synthesized/placeholder nodes.

line.description: 1-based line number of the node in `file`. Omitted on synthesized/placeholder nodes that have no single source location.

model.description: Optional model/type name associated with a synthesized group (e.g. the contract state type that drives Threat Models).

note.description: Optional human-readable note about how this node was produced or why it is a placeholder.

children.description: Child nodes. Present ONLY on group nodes; its absence is the signal that a node is a leaf. Leaf nodes (kind "test" or "placeholder") MUST NOT carry a `children` key at all. Enforced by the conditional (if kind=="group" then children is required, else children is forbidden).
